title: malpighiaceae
intro: The Malpighiaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the Barbados cherry or Malpighiaceae family. This family consists of approximately 130 genera and over 1,300 species of plants, many of which are native to the Americas, Africa, and Asia. The Malpighiaceae family is characterized by its diverse range of plant species, including trees, shrubs, and vines, and is often found in tropical and subtropical regions. The unique characteristics of the Malpighiaceae family have led to its classification as a distinct group within the plant kingdom.
